description: You are acting as a localization engineer who is about to start updating the Russian translation of an application. Before touching the file, you want a quick *diagnostic snapshot* that tells you how many messages are still **un-translated**. An untranslated message in a GNU gettext “*.po*” file is any entry whose msgstr is still the empty string (msgstr ""). Your task 1. Count how many empty-string msgstr lines are currently present in the file /home/user/project/locale/ru.po 2. Write that single integer value to a new text file called /home/user/project/diagnostics/untranslated_ru.log Output file format (must match exactly; the automated test is case-sensitive): • The file must contain exactly one line, no leading or trailing spaces. • The line must be UNTRANSLATED_STRINGS=<number> where <number> is the count you obtained. • Make sure there is a single trailing newline character (the usual Unix “\n”). Example (for illustration only): UNTRANSLATED_STRINGS=17⏎ Hints • A single terminal command using tools such as grep -c '^msgstr ""' or awk is enough; you do not need to open an editor. • Create the diagnostics directory if it is not already present. The automated checker will look only for: • the existence of /home/user/project/diagnostics/untranslated_ru.log • the presence of exactly one line in that file, formatted as described above, containing the correct count.

  Expected final state after successful task completion:

  • The file /home/user/project/diagnostics/untranslated_ru.log exists.
  • Its content is exactly:

    (where “\n” denotes the single trailing newline character)
